neighborhoods.name: Center City
neighborhoods.description: Philadelphia's downtown core between the Delaware and Schuylkill rivers, with high-rise living, cultural venues, and major transit hubs
neighborhoods.character: Urban core, highly walkable, culturally dense, transit-rich

housing.predominantType: High-rise condos, luxury apartments, and converted loft spaces

neighborhoods.character_notes: SEPTA bus, subway, and trolley all converge here. Reading Terminal Market for daily shopping. Broad Street is the cultural spine with theaters and concert halls.

neighborhoods.name: Rittenhouse Square
neighborhoods.description: Philadelphia's most prestigious residential neighborhood surrounding the elegant Rittenhouse Square park, with upscale dining and boutiques

housing.predominantType: Luxury condos, historic brownstones, and pre-war apartment buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The square itself is a social gathering place year-round. Farmer's market on Saturdays. Among the highest property values in Philadelphia. Fine dining concentration.

neighborhoods.name: Chestnut Hill
neighborhoods.description: Affluent neighborhood in northwest Philadelphia with a walkable main street, independent shops, and a village-in-the-city atmosphere
neighborhoods.character: Village-like, leafy, affluent, strong community identity

housing.predominantType: Large stone and stucco houses, some dating to the 1700s, plus newer townhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: SEPTA regional rail and bus service to Center City. Germantown Avenue offers boutique shopping and dining. Wissahickon Valley Park is adjacent for hiking.

neighborhoods.name: Manayunk
neighborhoods.description: Former mill town along the Schuylkill River and canal, now a vibrant neighborhood with Main Street restaurants, bars, and shops
neighborhoods.character: Hilly, lively Main Street, young-professional energy, river views

housing.predominantType: Rowhouses on steep hills, converted mill buildings, and newer condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: Very steep streets -- consider mobility before choosing. Manayunk Towpath along the canal is great for walking and cycling. SEPTA Manayunk/Norristown line stops here.

neighborhoods.name: Old City
neighborhoods.description: Philadelphia's oldest neighborhood near Independence Hall and the Liberty Bell, blending colonial history with galleries, restaurants, and loft living

housing.predominantType: Converted warehouse lofts, modern condos, and historic rowhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: First Friday art walks draw crowds monthly. Elfreth's Alley is the oldest residential street in America. Close to I-95 and the Ben Franklin Bridge to New Jersey.
